channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Cozy Gaming: A Refuge from Turbulent Times
item.description: Discover the world of cozy gaming, where games offer a comforting escape from reality.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Beware of Black Friday frenzy – UK issues an "urgent alert" as shopping scams surge
item.description: Brits lost over £11.5 million in online shopping scams in 2023. Here's all you need to know to avoid shopping scams this Black Friday.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Crypto, online betting, and vaping: Welcome to the Trump economy
item.description: From tariffs and Big Tech to impacts on the free market—everyone wants to know what Donald Trump’s return to office means for the business world. To cut through the noise, political scientist Ian Bremmer offers his insights.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX will deliver its first lunar terrain vehicle to the Moon by 2029
item.description: Hot on the heels of another productive test flight, two new lunar customers have been added to the flight manifest for SpaceX’s Starship. Eagle landing Lunar Outpost said on Thursday that SpaceX will deliver its first Lunar Terrain Vehicle, Eagle, to the Moon by 2029.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: Gallagher not interested in serving as SEC chair in second Trump administration
item.description: Dan Gallagher, chief legal officer at the financial services firm Robinhood, said Friday that he is not interested in chairing the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in President-elect Trump’s second administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists discover 4,000-year-old canals used to fish by predecessors of ancient Maya
item.description: Using drones and Google Earth imagery, archaeologists have discovered a 4,000-year-old network of earthen canals in what’s now Belize.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI is thinking about building its own browser
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly thinking about developing its own browser with the aim of challenging Google’s dominance in the market, according to The Information. The new browser would have built-in support for Chat GPT and Open AI’s search engine Search GPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple readies more conversational Siri in bid to catch up in AI
item.description: Apple has been testing the upgraded software on iPhones, iPads and Macs as a separate app, but the technology will ultimately replace the Siri interface that users rely on today.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple plans for a smarter LLM-based Siri smart assistant
item.description: Once upon a time, we’d say software is eating the planet. It still is, but these days our world is being consumed by generative AI (genAI), which is seemingly being added to everything.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: NetBox Labs launches tools to combat network configuration drift
item.description: NetBox Labs is expanding its network management platform this week with a pair of new products designed to tackle the growing challenges of infrastructure documentation and configuration management.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is a data architect? Skills, salaries, and how to become a data framework master
item.description: Data architect role Data architects are senior visionaries who translate business requirements into technology requirements, and define data standards and principles, often in support of data or digital transformations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dell shares its vision of the AI factory powered by NVIDIA
item.description: Factories have been the bedrock of many industries from manufacturing to automotive. But just as factories have fueled the industrial revolution, a new structure will be powering a new transformation in the age of AI: AI factories.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nile unwraps NaaS security features for enterprise customers
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added new zero-trust capabilities and tapped Microsoft and Palo Alto Networks to bolster its enterprise security services. Nile was founded by former Cisco CEO John Chambers and Pankaj Patel, Cisco’s former chief development officer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: MLB will test robot umpires at 13 spring training ballparks hosting 19 teams
item.description: MLB has been experimenting with the automated ball-strike system in the minor leagues since 2019 but is still working on the shape of the strike zone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: SEC chair to step down on Inauguration Day
item.description: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Gary Gensler will step down from his role when President-elect Trump takes office on Jan. 20, he announced Thursday. “The Securities and Exchange Commission is a remarkable agency,” Gensler said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How to protect your deliveries from getting stolen by porch pirates
item.description: Porch pirates are on the prowl this holiday season. Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson has a number of tips to prevent holiday package thefts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta's Threads app launches custom feeds amid Bluesky surge
item.description: Meta's Instagram Threads began rolling out a new custom feed feature to give users the ability to build their feeds around the people and topics they are most interested in.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple Responds to Zero-Day Attacks with Patches - Update Now!
item.description: Apple has released patches for Intel Macs, iPhones, iPads, and Vision Pro users following zero-day attacks targeting Apple devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How your browser is spying on you: Hidden dangers lurking behind every click
item.description: Internet browsers are useful, but they can be dangerous. Clicking on malicious links can direct you to dangerous websites that steal information or infect your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: F# 9 adds nullable reference types
item.description: F# 9, the latest version of Microsoft’s open source functional language, has been released with support for nullable reference types and empty-bodied computation expressions. Standard library improvements also are featured, including random sampling functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: T-Mobile hacked by Chinese cyber espionage in major attack on US telecoms
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about T-Mobile being hacked in broad cyberattack on global phone and internet companies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation
